name: Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952
officialCitation: The Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952 (Act No. 19 of 1952), repealed and saved under section 164 of the Code on Social Security, 2020.
overview: Historical central labour-law record governing provident fund, pension and deposit-linked insurance coverage before the Code on Social Security, 2020 became operative on 21 November 2025. The Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952 was repealed by section 164 of the Code, subject to savings. Current provident fund obligations must be assessed under Chapter III of the Code on Social Security, 2020, together with applicable schemes, rules, regulations, notifications and EPFO directions. The Employees' Provident Funds Scheme, 1952, Employees' Pension Scheme, 1995 and Employees' Deposit Linked Insurance Scheme, 1976 continue during the statutory savings period to the extent that they are not inconsistent with the Code. This record is retained for historical periods, saved instruments, exemptions, proceedings and audit traceability.

definitions.definition: An establishment to which the Act applies in accordance with the Act and notifications.
An employee covered under the Act as applicable.
The person or organisation responsible for compliance under the Act.

rules.title: Review EPF Applicability
rules.description: Determine whether the establishment is required to obtain EPF registration based on applicable legal criteria.

citation.reference: Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952

rules.title: Employer Registration
rules.description: Where applicable, complete employer registration with EPFO.

rules.title: Employee Enrollment
rules.description: Enroll eligible employees under the EPF scheme.

rules.title: Monthly Contributions
rules.description: Deposit employer and employee provident fund contributions within the prescribed timelines.

rules.title: Maintain EPF Records
rules.description: Maintain prescribed employee records and contribution records.

exceptions.title: Voluntary Coverage
exceptions.description: Certain establishments may seek voluntary coverage subject to applicable provisions.

exceptions.title: Saved Schemes and Exemptions
exceptions.description: The Employees' Provident Funds Scheme, 1952, Employees' Pension Scheme, 1995, Employees' Deposit Linked Insurance Scheme, 1976 and existing exemptions continue only to the extent and for the periods preserved by section 164 of the Code on Social Security, 2020.

recommendations.title: Retain Historical EPF Records
recommendations.description: Preserve registration, UAN, wage, contribution, exemption, assessment and proceeding records created under the repealed Act for saved liabilities and audit traceability.

recommendations.title: Use the Social Security Code Record for Current Compliance
recommendations.description: Assess current provident fund obligations under CENTRAL-SOCIAL-SECURITY while continuing to follow saved EPFO schemes, regulations, notifications and operational directions during the statutory transition.

sources.name: Code on Social Security, 2020 and Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952
sources.authority: Legislative Department, Ministry of Law and Justice, Government of India

governance.changeSummary: Updated the Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act record as a superseded historical record, preserved the existing definitions, rule IDs and voluntary-coverage exception, documented the statutory savings for EPF, pension and deposit-linked insurance schemes, and linked current provident fund compliance to the Code on Social Security, 2020 from 21 November 2025.
